suppressedMessages.message:
Compilation Skipped: Use of incompatible library

This API returns functions which cannot be memoized without leading to stale UI. To prevent this, by default React Compiler will skip memoizing this component/hook. However, you may see issues if values from this API are passed to other components/hooks that are memoized.

/home/hoangnq/ai-driven/product-app/logitrack/logitrack-frontend/src/app/admin/drivers/data-table.tsx:29:19
  27 | }: DataTableProps<TData, TValue>) {
  28 |     // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/incompatible-library
> 29 |     const table = useReactTable({
     |                   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^ TanStack Table's `useReactTable()` API returns functions that cannot be memoized safely
  30 |         data,
  31 |         columns,
  32 |         getCoreRowModel: getCoreRowModel(),

messages.message: Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

suggestions.desc: Use `unknown` instead, this will force you to explicitly, and safely assert the type is correct.
Use `never` instead, this is useful when instantiating generic type parameters that you don't need to know the type of.

messages.message: 'newStatus' is assigned a value but never used.

suppressedMessages.message: React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'startTracking'. Either include it or remove the dependency array.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [isOnline, driver, startTracking]
